dominant_theme: Dependency conflicts and version incompatibilities plague AI framework stability across ecosystems.
summary: AI workflow tools face critical stability issues driven by dependency management failures, async/streaming timeouts, and version incompatibilities. LangChain, OpenAI, and LLaMA Index frameworks struggle with breaking changes, particularly around tenacity library updates and async operations. Structured output parsing and callback mechanisms frequently fail after upgrades. Multi-model support and non-OpenAI provider integration remain underdeveloped, forcing developers to work around limitations.

painpoints.title: Dependency version conflicts halt development
painpoints.description: Multiple frameworks experience breaking changes from transitive dependencies. Tenacity 8.4.0 and httpx 0.28.0 updates introduce incompatibilities that lock developers into older versions. Llama Index creates circular dependency constraints making it impossible to upgrade without framework intervention.
sample_quotes: Constant timeouts after multiple asynchronous calls
It's literally impossible to fix this issue without intervention from LLAMA INDEX team
Tenacity library recently updated to v8.4.0 and they had broken some of the imports

painpoints.title: Async/streaming operations fail after 5 minutes
painpoints.description: Long-running API calls consistently timeout with connection errors. Streaming output from GPT-4 fails with 'InvalidChunkLength' errors for extended responses. Async operations using LLaMA Index framework experience repeated timeouts.
sample_quotes: Streaming output from gpt4 consistently fails with Connection broken: InvalidChunkLength
Constant timeouts after multiple asynchronous calls
Stream error after 5 minutes

painpoints.title: Breaking changes between minor version upgrades
painpoints.description: Framework updates introduce unannounced breaking changes that disable core functionality. ChatCompletionToolParam becomes invalid, callback trackers stop working, and import statements fail. Developers forced to pin exact versions rather than accepting semantic versioning.
sample_quotes: get_openai_callback not working when using Agent Executor after updating to latest version
This bug fix commit introduces a breaking change in 1.99.2
Regression: ChatCompletionToolParam no longer a valid type

painpoints.title: Structured output parsing returns wrong types
painpoints.description: Structured output features inconsistently return strings instead of Pydantic models. Advanced schema definitions fail to parse correctly. Response format validation lacks robustness for edge cases.
sample_quotes: Structured Output sometimes return string instead of Pydantic Model
Structured Output doesn't work with advanced schema

painpoints.title: Streaming and real-time output generation unsupported
painpoints.description: Agent frameworks lack streaming capabilities for real-time message delivery. Multi-agent systems cannot stream responses incrementally, forcing buffering of complete responses. Streaming function calls remain unimplemented across major frameworks.
sample_quotes: Streaming support
Move print out of client.py for streaming
Add support to streaming function call

painpoints.title: Multi-LLM provider support severely limited
painpoints.description: Non-OpenAI model integration remains incomplete and unreliable. Bedrock Converse Chat breaks between patch versions, Google PaLM API adoption stalled, and Golang/Rust implementations unavailable. Vendor lock-in to OpenAI persists despite roadmap commitments.
sample_quotes: Bedrock Converse Chat with Tools is throwing error in latest release 0.4.2
Enhanced non-OpenAI models
Introducing Rust and Golang implementations

painpoints.title: Source attribution and retrieval transparency broken
painpoints.description: RetrievalQA chains provide unreliable source attribution, hiding which documents informed the response. Transparency into retrieval pipeline lost, making it impossible to audit or correct model reasoning based on sources.
sample_quotes: RetrievalQAWithSourcesChain provides unreliable sources
The sources component of the output is not providing transparency into what documents the retriever

painpoints.title: Callback and monitoring functions silently fail
painpoints.description: OpenAI callback mechanisms stop functioning after framework updates. Cost tracking and token counting features disabled without error messages, leaving developers unaware monitoring has broken. Callback integration fragile across framework versions.
sample_quotes: get_openai_callback not working when using Agent Executor

painpoints.title: Framework installation and dependency resolution fails
painpoints.description: Framework setup fails with missing optional dependencies and broken dependency chains. AutoGen UI startup fails due to flaml[automl] requirement. Circular dependencies create impossible constraint resolution scenarios.
sample_quotes: autogenstudio fails to start
flaml.automl is not available
LLAMA INDEX is becoming a Dependency Hell itself

painpoints.title: Tool execution workflow lacks flexibility
painpoints.description: AutoGen's tool execution paradigm heavily relies on code generation, lacking direct execution pathways. Simple tool calling requires heavy framework overhead. Alternative execution patterns unavailable for lightweight use cases.
sample_quotes: Autogen heavily works on the concept of generating code and executing it
I currently just need a simple tool executor
